LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT YI TAN'S MOTION TO VACATE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT. THE COURT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING ON THE 2 MOTIONS ON CALENDAR. DEFENDANT YI TAN'S MOTION TO VACATE DEFAULT AND DEFAULT JUDGMENT IS GRANTED. THE DEFAULT ENTERED AGAINST MR. TAN ON APRIL 18, 2018 AND THE DEFAULT JUDGMENT ENTERED AGAINST HIM ON JULY 17, 2018 ARE SET ASIDE PER CCP 473.5 AND 473(B). MR. TAN HAS SHOWN THAT HE DID NOT HAVE ACTUAL KNOWLEDGE OF THIS LAWSUIT IN TIME TO FILE A TIMELY RESPONSIVE PLEADING AND THAT, EVEN IF THERE WERE COMMUNICATIONS DIRECTED TO HIM ABOUT THE LAWSUIT PRIOR TO ENTRY OF THE DEFAULT, HIS FAILURE TO REALIZE THE NATURE OF THOSE COMMUNICATIONS AND HIS FAILURE TO RESPOND TO THE COMPLAINT WAS A RESULT OF MISTAKE, INADVERTENCE, SURPRISE AND/OR EXCUSABLE NEGLECT. IN ADDITION, THE COURT ORALLY GRANTS A TEMPORARY ATTACHMENT AS TO FUNDS HELD BY THE COURT. (SEE MINI MINUTES FOR DETAILS) ALSO, THE PARTIES AGREE ON A BRIEFING SCHEDULE ON THE ANTICIPATED WRIT OF ATTACHMENT THAT THE PLAINTIFF WILL FILE. HEARING ON 1/10/19, PLAINTIFF'S BRIEF IS DUE ON 11/30/18, DEFENDANT'S OPPOSITION IS DUE ON 12/10/18. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER THAT IS VERBATIM WITH THE COURT'S RULING IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, CLERK: M. Notice Of Motion And Motion To Vacate Default And Default Judgment
Off Calendar Nov-01-2018 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Bahrm Seyedin-Noor 415-868-5602 and Daniel Sakaguchi, Esq. 415-766-3584 of Alto Litigation for Plaintiff.
Josh Escovedo, Esq. 916-558-6181 of Weintraub Tobin for Defendant YI TAN.
Robert Mazzola, Esq. 949-209-7603 of Waterstone Edge Specially appearing for Other party Inception Capital Management.
Brian Lee, Esq. 650-251-9200 for Other party Edwin Li.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court shares it's views. Plaintiff does not contest due process and public policy. Plaintiff argues actual notice. The Court suggests to continue the motion on the charging order until they can file an attachment motion. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:
DEFENDANT YI TAN's Motion To Vacate Default And Default Judgment.
The Court adopts the tentative ruling on the 2 motions on calendar. Defendant Yi Tan's motion to vacate default and default judgment is granted. The default entered against Mr. Tan on April 18, 2018 and the default judgment entered against him on July 17, 2018 are set aside per CCP 473.5 and 473(b). Mr. Tan has shown that he did not have actual knowledge of this lawsuit in time to file a timely responsive pleading and that, even if there were communications directed to him about the lawsuit prior to entry of the default, his failure to realize the nature of those communications and his failure to respond to the complaint was a result of mistake, inadvertence, surprise and/or excusable neglect. Plaintiffs have not provided persuasive evidence rebutting Mr. Tan's denial of knowledge about this case prior to entry of the default judgment and his denials of making any efforts to evade service of the summons and complaint. Granting this motion comports with California's strong policy of deciding cases on their merits and plaintiffs will not suffer legal prejudice to their ability to prosecute their claims by the granting of the motion. The court does not rule on and reserves the issue of whether some or all of the costs incurred by plaintiffs up to this point in this case should be borne by Mr. Tan. Plaintiffs are permitted to pursue discovery seeking information about Mr. Tan's knowledge of this case prior to the entry of the default judgment and any efforts by Mr. Tan to evade service of the summons and complaint. If plaintiffs believe they have compelling evidence that Mr. Tan knew about this case prior to the entry of the default judgment and/or made efforts to evade service of the summons and complaint, in addition to seeking reconsideration of this order or inviting the court to reconsider the order, they may also seek an award of some or all of the costs they incurred up to this point in the case.
In addition, the Court orally grants a temporary attachment as to funds held by the Court. Also, the Parties agree on a briefing schedule on the anticipated writ of attachment that the Plaintiff will file. Hearing on 1/10/19, Plaintiff's brief is due on 11/30/18, Defendant's Opposition is due on 12/10/18.
Prevailing party to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.
Judge: Harold Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Anthony Vaughn, CSR#6185, tonyvaughn@yahoo.com, Reported. =(302/HK)
